description: reproduced baseline, attributed modules, semantic integration, falsifiable novelty, and fair experiments

constraints: one 8 GB GPU
fixed simulator and evaluation code
p95 decision latency increase below 15 percent
all source records are synthetic evaluation fixtures

expected_results.guardrail: task success must not decrease by more than 0.02
the gain must remain positive on shifted scenarios
ontology maintenance time is included
latency remains within 15 percent of baseline

task.intended_claim: an uncertainty-triggered, action-constrained residual policy improves robustness and reduces adjustment work without sacrificing task success
task.mechanism_hypothesis: shift raises policy uncertainty while the unconstrained action head has no explicit representation of scenario-valid actions

module_intents.adapter: a named action identifier join checks ontology coverage before filtering
assumptions: the action ontology is versioned
every baseline action identifier resolves before masking
module_intents.baseline_parity_behavior: disabling enable_semantic_action_mask preserves frozen baseline logits and action selection
module_intents.failure_mode: an incomplete ontology may suppress a necessary action
module_intents.gradient_expectation: no gradient is introduced by the deterministic mask
module_intents.host_input_semantics: ordered action scores paired with a shared action ontology
module_intents.host_output_semantics: filtered scores over the unchanged action vocabulary
module_intents.insertion_point: the boundary after frozen action scores and before action selection
module_intents.loss_scale: not applicable because the module is non-trainable
module_intents.masks: binary scenario-valid action mask with explicit unknown-action rejection
module_intents.normalization: reuse frozen baseline logits before categorical normalization
module_intents.ordering: preserve the baseline action ontology order
module_intents.parameter_update_scope: no trainable parameters; baseline remains frozen
module_intents.predicted_effect: reduce intent violations caused by structurally invalid choices
module_intents.proposed_role: remove actions that are invalid in the current scenario
module_intents.semantic_mapping: the module uses the same action identifiers and ordering as the baseline

assumptions: uncertainty calibration uses only the training split
residual magnitude is bounded
module_intents.baseline_parity_behavior: disabling enable_uncertainty_residual returns the unchanged frozen baseline action values
module_intents.gradient_expectation: gradients update the residual and calibration heads but do not enter the frozen baseline encoder
module_intents.host_input_semantics: baseline features and ordered action values
module_intents.host_output_semantics: refined values with the same action order
module_intents.input_shape: batch x feature_dim plus batch x action_vocabulary
module_intents.insertion_point: the interface following baseline feature encoding
module_intents.loss_scale: residual loss weight 1.0 and calibration loss weight 0.1
module_intents.masks: respect the semantic action mask before residual action selection
module_intents.normalization: use frozen encoder statistics and temperature-calibrated uncertainty
module_intents.ordering: preserve the baseline action ontology order
module_intents.parameter_update_scope: residual policy and uncertainty calibration parameters only
module_intents.predicted_effect: improve task success in shifted scenarios
module_intents.semantic_mapping: source and host share action identifiers and value order

task.novelty_thesis: couple semantic action masking with uncertainty-triggered residual correction so the baseline is preserved in familiar states and changed only for high-risk decisions
task.observed_failure: a reproduced behavior-cloning policy works on familiar scenes but emits invalid or intent-inconsistent actions after distribution shift

papers.compute_cost: 4.8 ms median decision latency in the synthetic fixture
papers.input_semantics: ordered observation history plus explicit intent token
limitations: expert data covers only familiar scenario combinations
the action head does not encode scenario-invalid actions
papers.method_summary: a recurrent policy maps ordered observations and intent tokens to a categorical action distribution
papers.original_problem: imitate expert NPC trajectories in a fixed scenario distribution
papers.output_semantics: probability distribution over the complete discrete action vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: frozen recurrent observation encoder and categorical action head
papers.title: Synthetic Paper A: Reproducible Behavior Cloning for Multi-Intent NPC Control

papers.compute_cost: linear in the number of actions and below 0.5 ms in the fixture
papers.input_semantics: current scenario state and discrete action ontology
limitations: cannot identify semantically poor actions that remain technically valid
requires a maintained action ontology
papers.method_summary: a rule-derived binary mask removes invalid actions before categorical normalization
papers.original_problem: prevent structurally invalid actions in discrete control
papers.output_semantics: binary validity indicator for every action in the shared action vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: pre-normalization action-validity mask with explicit state-to-action semantics
papers.title: Synthetic Paper B: Semantic Invalid-Action Masking for Safe Policy Optimization

papers.compute_cost: approximately 8 percent additional decision latency in the fixture
papers.input_semantics: frozen policy features, logits, and calibrated uncertainty
limitations: poor calibration may activate corrections too often
residual learning can overfit rare failure states
papers.method_summary: a calibrated uncertainty score gates a low-capacity residual policy that corrects frozen policy logits
papers.original_problem: adapt a frozen controller only in uncertain states
papers.output_semantics: bounded correction over the same ordered action vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: calibrated uncertainty gate and bounded residual-logit correction
papers.title: Synthetic Paper C: Uncertainty-Gated Residual Policies under Distribution Shift

papers.compute_cost: approximately three times baseline inference cost
papers.input_semantics: the same ordered observations and intent tokens as the baseline
limitations: higher inference cost
does not explicitly remove scenario-invalid actions
papers.method_summary: an ensemble of independently trained behavior policies averages calibrated action values under shift
papers.original_problem: improve NPC control under scenario distribution shift
papers.output_semantics: calibrated action values over the same discrete vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: full comparator implementation and fixed evaluation recipe
papers.title: Synthetic Paper D: Shift-Robust Ensemble Policy for NPC Control

task.preprocessing: fixed observation normalization and action ontology version 1

reproduction.acceptance_tolerance: two percentage points or five percent relative
notes: all values are simulated evaluator fixtures
modules-disabled execution matches the frozen fixture baseline
no real experimental result is claimed
reproduction.split: familiar training and validation, familiar plus shifted testing

task.scientific_setting: single-agent game environments with partially observed state and changing scenario rules
stop_conditions: stop if the frozen baseline cannot be reproduced within tolerance
stop if a selected source is unverified or cannot be reused
stop if action identifiers or ordering cannot be aligned
stop if the full method does not reduce violations across four of five seeds
stop if p95 latency exceeds the 15 percent guardrail

strong_comparisons.contrast: full proposed method versus the attributed ensemble under the same shifted test split and tuning budget
strong_comparisons.name: shift-robust-ensemble-comparison
strong_comparisons.purpose: test whether the proposed mechanism adds value beyond a strong compute-heavier shift-robust alternative

task.target_problem: reduce unintended NPC actions and manual rule adjustment under scenario shift
task.tuning_budget: 20 trials per arm with the same early-stop budget
task.uncertainty_reporting: mean, standard deviation, and 95 percent bootstrap interval
task.why_not_simple_splice: the mask and residual policy address different mechanisms and are connected by an explicit uncertainty trigger rather than unconditional stacking

papers.input_semantics: ordered observation history plus explicit intent token
limitations: expert data covers only familiar scenario combinations
the action head does not encode scenario-invalid actions
papers.method_summary: a recurrent policy maps ordered observations and intent tokens to a categorical action distribution
papers.original_problem: imitate expert NPC trajectories in a fixed scenario distribution
papers.output_semantics: probability distribution over the complete discrete action vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: frozen recurrent observation encoder and categorical action head
papers.title: Synthetic Paper A: Reproducible Behavior Cloning for Multi-Intent NPC Control

papers.compute_cost: linear in the number of actions and below 0.5 ms in the fixture
papers.input_semantics: current scenario state and discrete action ontology
limitations: cannot identify semantically poor actions that remain technically valid
requires a maintained action ontology
papers.method_summary: a rule-derived binary mask removes invalid actions before categorical normalization
papers.original_problem: prevent structurally invalid actions in discrete control
papers.output_semantics: binary validity indicator for every action in the shared action vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: pre-normalization action-validity mask with explicit state-to-action semantics
papers.title: Synthetic Paper B: Semantic Invalid-Action Masking for Safe Policy Optimization

papers.compute_cost: approximately 8 percent additional decision latency in the fixture
papers.input_semantics: frozen policy features, logits, and calibrated uncertainty
limitations: poor calibration may activate corrections too often
residual learning can overfit rare failure states
papers.method_summary: a calibrated uncertainty score gates a low-capacity residual policy that corrects frozen policy logits
papers.original_problem: adapt a frozen controller only in uncertain states
papers.output_semantics: bounded correction over the same ordered action vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: calibrated uncertainty gate and bounded residual-logit correction
papers.title: Synthetic Paper C: Uncertainty-Gated Residual Policies under Distribution Shift

papers.compute_cost: approximately three times baseline inference cost
papers.input_semantics: the same ordered observations and intent tokens as the baseline
limitations: higher inference cost
does not explicitly remove scenario-invalid actions
papers.method_summary: an ensemble of independently trained behavior policies averages calibrated action values under shift
papers.original_problem: improve NPC control under scenario distribution shift
papers.output_semantics: calibrated action values over the same discrete vocabulary
papers.reusable_component: full comparator implementation and fixed evaluation recipe
papers.title: Synthetic Paper D: Shift-Robust Ensemble Policy for NPC Control

task.preprocessing: fixed observation normalization and action ontology version 1

reproduction.acceptance_tolerance: two percentage points or five percent relative

task.scientific_setting: single-agent game environments with partially observed state and changing scenario rules
stop_conditions: stop if the frozen baseline cannot be reproduced within tolerance
stop if a selected source is unverified or cannot be reused
stop if action identifiers or ordering cannot be aligned
stop if the full method does not reduce violations across four of five seeds
stop if p95 latency exceeds the 15 percent guardrail

strong_comparisons.contrast: full proposed method versus the attributed ensemble under the same shifted test split and tuning budget
strong_comparisons.name: shift-robust-ensemble-comparison
strong_comparisons.purpose: test whether the proposed mechanism adds value beyond a strong compute-heavier shift-robust alternative

task.target_problem: reduce unintended NPC actions and manual rule adjustment under scenario shift
task.tuning_budget: 20 trials per arm with the same early-stop budget
task.uncertainty_reporting: mean, standard deviation, and 95 percent bootstrap interval
task.why_not_simple_splice: the mask and residual policy address different mechanisms and are connected by an explicit uncertainty trigger rather than unconditional stacking

description: the claimed contribution is only that two modules are combined

constraints: one 8 GB GPU
fixed simulator and evaluation code
p95 decision latency increase below 15 percent
all source records are synthetic evaluation fixtures

expected_results.guardrail: task success must not decrease by more than 0.02
the gain must remain positive on shifted scenarios
ontology maintenance time is included
latency remains within 15 percent of baseline

task.intended_claim: an uncertainty-triggered, action-constrained residual policy improves robustness and reduces adjustment work without sacrificing task success
task.mechanism_hypothesis: shift raises policy uncertainty while the unconstrained action head has no explicit representation of scenario-valid actions

module_intents.adapter: a named action identifier join checks ontology coverage before filtering
assumptions: the action ontology is versioned
every baseline action identifier resolves before masking
module_intents.baseline_parity_behavior: disabling enable_semantic_action_mask preserves frozen baseline logits and action selection
module_intents.failure_mode: an incomplete ontology may suppress a necessary action
module_intents.gradient_expectation: no gradient is introduced by the deterministic mask
module_intents.host_input_semantics: ordered action scores paired with a shared action ontology
module_intents.host_output_semantics: filtered scores over the unchanged action vocabulary
module_intents.insertion_point: the boundary after frozen action scores and before action selection
module_intents.loss_scale: not applicable because the module is non-trainable
module_intents.masks: binary scenario-valid action mask with explicit unknown-action rejection
module_intents.normalization: reuse frozen baseline logits before categorical normalization
module_intents.ordering: preserve the baseline action ontology order
module_intents.parameter_update_scope: no trainable parameters; baseline remains frozen
module_intents.predicted_effect: reduce intent violations caused by structurally invalid choices
module_intents.proposed_role: remove actions that are invalid in the current scenario
module_intents.semantic_mapping: the module uses the same action identifiers and ordering as the baseline

assumptions: uncertainty calibration uses only the training split
residual magnitude is bounded
module_intents.baseline_parity_behavior: disabling enable_uncertainty_residual returns the unchanged frozen baseline action values
module_intents.gradient_expectation: gradients update the residual and calibration heads but do not enter the frozen baseline encoder
module_intents.host_input_semantics: baseline features and ordered action values
module_intents.host_output_semantics: refined values with the same action order
module_intents.input_shape: batch x feature_dim plus batch x action_vocabulary
module_intents.insertion_point: the interface following baseline feature encoding
module_intents.loss_scale: residual loss weight 1.0 and calibration loss weight 0.1
module_intents.masks: respect the semantic action mask before residual action selection
module_intents.normalization: use frozen encoder statistics and temperature-calibrated uncertainty
module_intents.ordering: preserve the baseline action ontology order
module_intents.parameter_update_scope: residual policy and uncertainty calibration parameters only
module_intents.predicted_effect: improve task success in shifted scenarios
module_intents.semantic_mapping: source and host share action identifiers and value order
